instruction: Design a birthday party plan for a 5-year-old, specifying games, snacks, and decorations. The plan must include at least three games, with one being a team-based activity. The letter "S" must appear exactly 15 times throughout the response. The entire response must be written in English and include a relevant English idiom. Maintain a cheerful and positive tone throughout the response. The phrase "fun for everyone" must appear exactly twice. The response must contain exactly 10 sentences, with each paragraph containing at least 3 sentences. Exclude any team-based activities from the games.
llm_response:
Planning a sweet surprise brings smiles to every single soul.
We will set up a space that shines with stars and suns.
This special day ensures fun for everyone without any fuss.

Three simple games will keep the little ones busy and happy.
First, they can sort soft balls into separate shiny boxes.
Next, they will search for hidden stars under the green grass.
Finally, they can spin a silly top until it stops.

A team-based activity is not part of this specific plan today.
Instead, each child plays alone to build their own joy.
This approach guarantees fun for everyone in the room.

Snacks like strawberries and sunny side-up eggs will taste great.
We will serve sweet sodas and soft sandwiches for lunch.
Every bite should bring a big smile to each guest.

The decorations will sparkle with silver streamers and blue balloons.
A big banner will say "Happy Birthday" in bright red letters.
Candles will glow softly as the song begins to play.

Remember that a stitch in time saves nine for good planning.
We must start early so the party runs smooth and sweet.
Preparation makes the magic happen for all the young stars.

Let the laughter flow like a river through the sunny park.
Each moment will be a treasure to remember forever.
We hope this plan creates a memory that lasts a lifetime.
evaluation_result:
**Analysis**: The instruction contains several conflicting constraints: it requires "one being a team-based activity" but later states "Exclude any team-based activities from the games." The model chose to follow the exclusion constraint ("A team-based activity is not part of this specific plan today") and provided a response. However, the model failed to adhere to the strict quantitative constraints regarding the letter "S" (which appears much more than 15 times), the exact number of sentences (it provided 21 sentences instead of 10), and the exact count of the phrase "fun for everyone" (it appeared twice, which was correct, but the other constraints were ignored). Since the model identified the conflict (the team-based activity) and resolved it by choosing one side to provide a response, it fits category 3.

**Answer**: 3

Conflicts between Content Constraints.new_constraint: Exclude any team-based activities from the games.
Conflicts between Content Constraints.org_constraint: The plan must include a team-based activity.
